title: Proxy-Masked Card Testing with Sequential Geographic Drift
narrative: Fraudster uses rotating proxy infrastructure to mask location while testing stolen card portfolios. Initial transactions appear from consistent IP geolocation (dist1 >5000km from billing addr1), but sequential transactions show impossible geographic drift patternsâ€”appearing from Seattle, then Miami, then Phoenix within 90 minutes despite identical device fingerprint. Tests start with low-value digital goods ($1-$15, ProductCD='W') to validate cards, then escalate to $200-$800 purchases if successful. email_to_IP_dist values exceed 3000km consistently, while C_emaildomain remains stable (gmail/yahoo). Card velocity shows 8-12 transactions across 4+ distinct dist1 values within 6-hour window, but device_id and UserAgent remain constant, indicating same actor behind proxy rotation.
indicators: dist1 values change by >2000km between consecutive transactions within 2-hour windows
card1_txn_count_1hr shows 8-12 transactions with 4+ distinct IP geolocations
ProductCD sequence: 3+ 'W' (digital) transactions under $15, then sudden jump to 'C'/'H' over $200
device_id and UserAgent remain identical despite dist1 geographic impossibilities
TransactionAmt escalation: $1-$15 range for first 2-4 txns, then $200+ within same session
addr1 to IP country mismatch maintained across 100% of transaction sequence
summary: The same device rapidly makes purchases from impossible locations—Seattle, Miami, Phoenix within 90 minutes—while email-to-IP distances stay above 3000km. Transactions start with small digital goods under $15, then jump to $200-800 purchases using the same cards.
reasoning: The fraudster rotates proxy servers to hide their real location while testing stolen cards with low-value purchases first. Geographic impossibilities combined with consistent device fingerprints reveal someone deliberately masking their identity to validate and exploit stolen payment credentials.
indicator_explanations: Card location jumps thousands of kilometers between purchases within hours
Email owner's location is consistently far from transaction IP addresses
Card racks up transactions from multiple countries in single hour
Small digital purchases suddenly escalate to large physical goods orders
Same device fingerprint appears despite physically impossible location changes
Transaction amounts climb from tiny tests to major purchases rapidly
Billing address country never matches any transaction location throughout session
